label: REITs (P/FFO + EV/EBITDAre approximations)

deferred_concepts.reason: Adjusted FFO requires recurring_capex + straight-line-rent adjustments — REIT supplemental disclosure, not standard XBRL
Requires PaymentsOfDividends concept fetch — deferred to v2.2.0-c-followup-divs
Net Operating Income — REIT supplemental segment disclosure
Requires NOI estimate plus EV — derived from deferred NOI

notes: P/FFO uses approximation FFO ≈ net_income + D&A. Strict NAREIT FFO subtracts gains_on_sale_of_property; that concept is not in standard US-GAAP XBRL → omitted. EV/EBITDAre uses approximation EBITDAre ≈ EBITDA (no impairment / gains_on_sale add-back). Both approximations are documented in per-multiple compute_provenance.note. trailingPE intentionally absent — REIT GAAP earnings are heavily dragged by D&A on real estate (which doesn't economically depreciate the same way machinery does), making P/E systematically low and hard to compare; FFO is the canonical REIT analogue. forwardPE consensus also unreliable for the same reason. Dividend yield (deferred) is critical for REIT valuation but requires a memo-fetch dividend-disclosure pass.
